text:
To determine how much money Sam would have to spend to make 4 liters of juice, we can break down the problem into a few simple steps.

1. First, calculate how much oranges Sam needs for 4 liters of juice.

   - Sam needs 5 kilograms of oranges for 1 liter of juice.
   - Therefore, for 4 liters of juice, he will need \( 5 \text{ kg/liter} \times 4 \text{ liters} = 20 \text{ kg} \).

2. Next, calculate the total cost of the oranges.

   - Each kilogram of oranges costs $3.
   - Therefore, for 20 kilograms of oranges, the total cost would be \( 20 \text{ kg} \times \$3/\text{kg} = \$60 \).

So, Sam would have to spend \$60 to make 4 liters of juice.
